data.title: Tom Lee: The deleveraging cycle in the crypto market may be nearing its end.
data.description: In an interview with CNBC, BitMine Chairman Tom Lee stated that the current weakness in the crypto market is highly similar to the crash of October 10th, when a stablecoin pricing error triggered the largest liquidation in history, resulting in nearly 2 million accounts being wiped out and liquidity drying up instantly. Lee pointed out that such deleveraging cycles typically last about 8 weeks, and we are currently in the 6th week, suggesting the market may be nearing the end of its correction.

data.title: Bitwise XRP ETF launched, with US XRP spot ETFs seeing a net inflow of $118 million in a single day.
data.description: On November 20th, Eastern Time, the Bitwise XRP ETF (ticker symbol XRP) officially listed on the NYSE after receiving SEC approval. According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (November 20th, Eastern Time), the total net inflow into XRP spot ETFs was $118 million. Specifically, the Bitwise XRP ETF saw a net inflow of $105 million on its first day of trading, with a trading volume of $26.41 million and a total net asset value of $108 million. The Canary XRPC ETF saw a single-day net inflow of $12.8 million, with a historical total net inflow of $305 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of XRP spot ETFs is $385 million, with an XRP net asset ratio of 0.32%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $411 million. The Bitwise XRP ETF supports cash or in-kind redemption and has a management fee of 0.34%.

data.title: Alliance Co-Founder Reiterates Caution on Cryptocurrency Market
data.description:
According to Odaily, Alliance co-founder @QwQiao has recently reiterated his warning about the cryptocurrency market, expressing strong agreement with his previous predictions. He had previously cautioned that the next bear market could be more severe than most anticipate, primarily due to the influx of uninformed investors purchasing digital asset trading platforms (DAT) and ETF products.

He believes this situation "never ends well" and forecasts that the market might need to undergo a roughly 50% correction before these investors exit their positions. Only then can the market establish a solid foundation and resume its supercycle growth. Although he did not specify a timeline, his reaffirmation indicates his firm stance on this prediction.

data.title: XRP New Today: XRP Dips 6% to Hover Around $2 Amid Whale Sell-Off
data.description: Key TakeawaysXRP price tumbles 6% to hover around $2.00, extending its multi-day weakness and reflecting bearish momentumWhale distribution accelerates, with 200M XRP offloaded in 48 hours, creating strong selling pressureBitwise XRP ETF closes 7% lower on debut, adding to ETF-driven volatilityXRP Declines As Whale Sell-Off IntensifiesXRP slipped 4.75% to $2.02 in the last 24 hours, underperforming the broader crypto market’s –4.69% move. Blockchain data shows whales distributed nearly 200M XRP (roughly $404M) over the past two days, adding significant selling pressure that outweighed buyer demand.This activity follows XRP’s 33% 90-day decline, signaling that larger holders may be reducing exposure amid continued downside risk. Rising exchange reserves further reinforce a cautious tone as more tokens move into liquid markets, a typical precursor to heightened volatility. ETF Debut Adds Volatility Instead of Boosting SentimentThe Bitwise XRP ETF, launched on Nov. 20, recorded $21.7M in turnover but closed the session 7% lower, reflecting a soft reception relative to the sector. Meanwhile, Canary Capital’s XRPC ETF, with $268M AUM, continues to dominate interest, raising questions about Bitwise’s ability to attract institutional participation.Weakness across traditional markets added to the pressure, with the Dow Jones falling –0.84%. Market sentiment remains “extreme fear” with a reading of 15, while rising Bitcoin dominance (58.16%) signals a broader shift into perceived safer crypto assets. Technical Structure Shows Further Downside RiskXRP broke below the $2.04 pivot and is now retesting the critical $2.00 Fibonacci (78.6%) support. The RSI at 33 signals oversold conditions but shows no bullish divergence, suggesting the downtrend may persist.A confirmed breakdown below $2.00 would extend the descending triangle pattern that formed after XRP fell from the $3 psychological level. The pattern’s structure points toward a measured move to $1.55, representing a possible 25% decline from current levels.The pattern confirmation occurred when XRP slid below $2.20 earlier this week. Historical analysis also warned of potential declines toward $1.61, reinforcing the current bearish setup.Source: TradingViewAdding to the overhead pressure, nearly 3.23B XRP were acquired between $2.38–$2.40, creating a supply cluster aligned marginally below the 100-day SMA, a stiff resistance zone that continues to cap upward momentum. Outlook: Can XRP Defend The $2.00 Support?The key question is whether XRP can hold $2.00, a psychologically and technically important level. A sustained break could expose the token to the $1.98 range and potentially toward the $1.55 triangle target.

data.title: Mu Digital has raised $1.5 million in Pre-Seed funding to focus on bringing high-yield credit from Asia onto the blockchain.
data.description: Mu Digital has announced the completion of a $1.5 million Pre-Seed funding round, with investors including UOB Venture Management, Signum Capital, CMS Holdings, Cointelegraph Accelerator, and Echo. Mu Digital focuses on bringing real-world assets from Asia's $20 trillion credit market onto the blockchain and plans to launch its Monad mainnet on November 24th. Products include Asia Dollar (AZND), offering yields of 6-7%, and muBOND, with yields up to 15%.

data.title: The crypto market saw a general correction, with BTC falling below $88,000, while the NFT and GameFi sectors remained relatively resilient.
data.description: On November 21st, according to SoSoValue data, the overall cryptocurrency market trended downward, with 24-hour declines generally ranging from 2% to 8%. Bitcoin (BTC) fell 5.71%, dropping below $88,000; Ethereum (ETH) fell 5.63%, dropping below $2,900. However, the GameFi and NFT sectors remained relatively resilient, rising 0.20% and 0.63% respectively. Within the GameFi sector, Beam (BEAM) rose 6.38%; within the NFT sector, Audiera (BEAT) surged 84.18%. In other sectors, the Layer 1 sector fell 3.79% in the last 24 hours, with NEAR Protocol (NEAR) down 12.36%; the CeFi sector fell 3.98%, with Aster (ASTER) down 9.93%; the Meme sector fell 4.25%, with Cheems Token (CHEEMS) bucking the trend and rising 10.40%; the DeFi sector fell 4.32%, with Uniswap (UNI) down 7.88%; the PayFi sector fell 4.82%, with Telcoin (TEL) down 19.39%; and the Layer 2 sector fell 5.05%, with SOON (SOON) down 17.72%. Crypto sector indices reflecting historical sector performance show that the ssiSocialFi, ssiDeFi, and ssiLayer2 indices fell 8.85%, 8.33%, and 7.15%, respectively.

data.title: JPMorgan Chase maintains a neutral rating on Bullish, but lowers its 2026 price target to $45.
data.description: According to The Block, JPMorgan Chase maintains a "neutral" rating on cryptocurrency exchange Bullish (BLSH), despite its better-than-expected Q3 results. However, it lowered its 2026 price target from $46 to $45, excluding stablecoin interest income from its IPO. The new target price represents approximately 23.3% upside from the current closing price of $36.5. Analysts expect increased market volatility in Q4 to improve the trading environment and are optimistic about the early launch of Bullish's options products and growth in institutional clients.

data.title: AVAX One Announces $40 Million Stock Buyback Plan
data.description: According to Odaily, Nasdaq-listed company AVAX One has announced that its board of directors has approved a one-year stock buyback program with a maximum limit of $40 million. CEO Jolie Kahn stated that the buyback at the current stock price level represents an efficient use of capital and demonstrates confidence in the company's long-term strategy and market value. AVAX One focuses on bridging the Avalanche ecosystem with traditional finance, aiming to build a digital asset vault and drive DeFi innovation.

data.title: A major whale's wstETH position was liquidated, worth approximately $6.52 million.
data.description: According to PeckShield monitoring, after ETH fell below $2,900, a whale's wstETH position was liquidated, worth approximately $6.52 million.

data.title: Alliance co-founders reiterated that the cryptocurrency market may face a pullback of over 50%.
data.description: Alliance co-founder @QwQiao recently reiterated his warnings about the cryptocurrency market, stating that he "pretty much" agrees with his previous predictions. He warned that the next bear market would be more severe than most people expect, primarily due to a large amount of "dumb money" with little knowledge of cryptocurrencies buying into digital asset trading platforms (DATs) and ETFs. He believes this situation "will never end well" and predicts the market may need to experience a 50% pullback before it can build a solid foundation and resume supercycle growth after these investors liquidate their positions. While he didn't give a specific timeframe, this reiteration demonstrates his continued conviction in this prediction.

data.title: Two people have been arrested in connection with the UK's Serious Fraud Office's investigation into the collapse of a $28 million crypto fund.
data.description: According to an official announcement, the UK's Serious Fraud Office (SFO) announced on Thursday that it is investigating the defunct crypto project Basis Markets, which abruptly shut down after raising approximately $28 million through NFT membership sales and token offerings at the end of 2021. Investigators have executed search warrants in London and West Yorkshire, arrested two men, and seized digital devices and documents. According to the SFO, Basis Markets had promised investors low-risk returns, but by June 2022, investors were told that "the project cannot proceed as planned due to newly proposed regulations in the US." The investigation suggests that funds may have been transferred directly to personal wallets controlled by an anonymous team. SFO Director Nick Ephgrave stated, "With our expanding capabilities in cryptocurrency investigations, we are determined to bring anyone attempting to defraud investors using cryptocurrencies to their advantage." The BASIS token fell by nearly 40% after the UK authorities announced the investigation.

data.title: A Bitcoin whale's short position has yielded a floating profit of over $57 million.

data.title: Whale Investor Reopens Long Positions Worth $23.19 Million
data.description: According to Foresight News, a prominent investor known as the 'whale' has reopened three long positions valued at approximately $23.19 million. The investments include 100,041.36 SOL at an average opening price of $134.36, 100.95 BTC at an average opening price of $88,189.6, and 11,071.24 LTC at an average opening price of $88.8.
